title: distimake dissectus var. edentatus
intro: Distimake dissectus var. edentatus, also known as the toothless distimake, is a variety of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, characterized by its lack of teeth on the margins of its leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Distimake dissectus var. edentatus include its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by drying the leaves and roots, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
